MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
1) PTR-17-300739, Augustina Clara Ballestrasse Survivor's Trust; and
2) PTR-24-307042, Augustina Clara Ballestrasse Bypass Trust.

Appearances:
Christopher Kelly appeared for temporary trustee Joanne Karetzky (via Zoom).
Jason Schwarz appeared for suspended trustee Paul Ballestrasse (via Zoom).

The matters were called for hearing on the First Amended Petitions for Accounting, Suspension and Removal, Surcharge, and Damages, and for status on the trusts and the accountings.

Mr. Kelly reported that accountings had not yet been filed in either matter. He stated that the accounting for the Survivor's Trust was overdue and that the accounting for the Bypass Trust had likewise not been provided. Mr. Kelly advised that Mr. Schwarz had recently been retained and that the parties were now able to communicate regarding how to proceed.

Mr. Schwarz confirmed that he represented Paul Ballestrasse in both matters. He reported that progress had been made toward preparation of the accountings, though corrections and revisions were still in progress. The Court noted that a substitution of attorney had not yet been filed in the Bypass Trust matter and advised that one should be filed to clarify the record.

The Court continued all matters to April 20, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court directed that accountings be prepared and filed in both matters on or before March 16, 2026.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called together:
1) PTR-17-300739, Augustina Clara Ballestrasse Survivor's Trust; and
2) PTR-24-307042, Augustina Clara Ballestrasse Bypass Trust.

Appearances:
Christopher Kelly for temporary trustee Joanne Karetzky (via Zoom).
Augustin Razo specially appeared for suspended trustee Paul Ballestrasse, on behalf of attorney James Arrasmith (via Zoom).
Jason Schwarz appeared as newly retained attorney for suspended trustee Paul Pallestrasse (via Zoom).

The Court took the Order to Appear off calendar in light of Christopher Kelly's appearance and the circumstances regarding the petitioner's change in representation.

Mr. Razo confirmed that Paul Ballestrasse has terminated the services of attorney James Arresmith, and that their appearance today was only to clarify that no substitution of attorney has yet been filed. Mr. Schwarz confirmed that he had recently been retained by Paul Ballestrasse and expects to be formally substituted in as his counsel.

Mr. Kelly stated that he had been unable to communicate with Respondent due to uncertainty over representation status, and that no responses had been received to recent outreach efforts. He noted this clarification was helpful and that progress had been delayed pending identification of counsel.

The Court asked how much time new counsel would need. Mr. Schwarz requested a continuance to early January and stated that he would meet and confer with opposing counsel. He also reported that a CPA is working on accounting issues.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to January 12, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called together:
1) PTR-17-300739, Augustina Clara Ballesrasse Survivor's Trust; and
2) PTR-24-307042, Augustina Clara Ballestrasse Bypass Trust.

Appearances:
Faith Hashemi for trustee Paul Ballestrasse (via Zoom).

Ms. Hashemi explained that her office was recently retained as counsel for Paul Ballestrasse and requested a continuance to allow time to familiarize with the case. The Court noted that no appearances were made by petitioner or their counsel.

After discussing scheduling, the Court continued the matter to November 17, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. An Order to Appear shall be issued to petitioner's attorney of record Christophe Nedeau to appear at the continued hearing.

MINI MINUTES: The following cases were called and heard together:
1) PTR-17-300739, In re: the Augustina Clara Ballestrasse Survivor's Trust; and
2) PTR-24-307042, In re: the Augustina Clara Ballestrasse Bypass Trust

Appearances:
Christopher Kelly for Joanne Karetzky, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jeff Neustadt, specially appearing for Paul Ballestrasse, Trustee.

The Court inquired whether any accounting had been received since the last hearing. Mr. Kelly confirmed that no accounting had been received. He explained that there were two main issues delaying progress: (1) the lack of an accounting from former trustee Paul Ballestrasse for the period during which he acted as trustee, and (2) complications surrounding the disposition of the primary trust asset, a property on 17th Street, which is currently the subject of a partition action with an unrelated third party.

Mr. Kelly noted that a status report had been filed on July 9 and indicated that if an accounting could not be obtained, the trustee might attempt to reconstruct the trust assets based on available records to move the matter forward.

Mr. Neustadt informed the Court that Mr. Ballestrasse had retained McKenzie Forensic Auditors to prepare the required accounting in compliance with the Court's prior orders. He stated that the accounting was nearly complete and would be finalized and shared with the Court and Mr. Kelly within 60 days.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to September 15, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

The following related cases are called jointly:
PTR-17-300739 and PTR-24-307042.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Christopher Kelly, Esq., for Joanne Karetsky, Petitioner.
Jeffrey Neustadt, in court, specially appearing for Paul Ballestrasse, Trustee.

At the outset, Mr. Nuestadt advises the court that Mr. Ballestrasse has been unable to prepare an accounting as ordered by the court due to health issues and confirms that he is requesting a continuance of ninety (90) days to allow Mr. Ballestrasse time to prepare said accountings and to continue to seek legal representation. Mr. Kelly advises of his client's position with additional assertions regarding the delays with the accountings. Mr. Kelly offers a status regarding the Bypass Trust's real property.

After additional discussion, all matters are continued to Jul-14-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

***After the court had stated the above continuance date and was ready to call the next matter, Mr. Ballestrasse appeared in person. The court repeated the continuance date. Mr. Ballestrasse acknowledged the new date and the court's expectations by the next calling.
Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Trust
Off Calendar Jan-27-2025 Continued To Apr-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Kelly, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, and Christophe Nedeua, in person, for Joanne Karetzky, Petitioner.
Paul Ballestrasse, in person, in propria persona, Trustee.

The court begins by confirming that Mr. Ballestrasse is now representing himself in this matter. Counsel and Mr. Ballestrasse presented oral argument to support their positions regarding this matter and the related Bypass Trust matter. There is discussion regarding the delay with the accountings that have been requested from the Trustee. The court further discusses the accounting issues with the Trustee. At the conclusion of discussion, the court confirms that it intends to suspend Paul Ballestrasse as Trustee of this Trust and the Bypass Trust and intends to appoint Joanne Karetsky as Trustee for the related Trusts. The court orders Paul Ballestrasse to provide an accounting from Jan-01-2017 to the present, forthwith. The court advises that it intends to issue an order after hearing to reaffirm the preceding.

The subject matter is continued to Apr-14-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Mar-11-2024 Continued To Apr-08-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (in person):
Christopher Nadeau, Esq., for Joanne Karetzky, Petitioner.
Jeffrey Neustadt, Esq., for Paul Ballestrasse, Trustee.

At the outset, counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the recently filed status report. Mr. Nedeau reports that hew has yet to be provided with a formal accounting. Mr. Neustadt reports that he submitted an accounting and schedules but that they had been rejected. The court advises Mr. Neustadt that it would like a formal response to the instant petition. The court instructs Mr. Neustadt to file amendments in this matter and the related bypass trust matter to address only the associated trust.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Apr-08-2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Trust
Off Calendar Mar-04-2024 Continued To Mar-11-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (In person):
Christopher Nedeau, Esq., for Joanne Karetzky, Petitioner.
Jeffrey Neustadt, Esq., for Paul Ballestrasse, Respondent.

At the outset, counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the subject matters and related bypass trust matter. Mr. Neustadt reports on the status of an accounting and requests a continuance to finalize it. Mr. Nedeau states in open court that he has a copy of the bypass trust and asks Mr. Neustadt to accept it on behalf of his client to avoid a delay in service of the same. Mr. Neustadt agrees to accept service of a copy of the bypass trust on behalf of his client in open court. There is discussion regarding whether Mr. Nedeau plans to file an amended petition in this case number. Counsel reports that a request to consolidate the related matters is included in the bypass Trust petition.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Mar-11-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The hearing on the related bypass trust matter, currently set for hearing on Apr-02-2024, is continued in open court to Apr-08-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Dec-18-2023 Continued To Mar-04-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Christopher Nedeau appeared in person for Joanne Karetzky (petitioner).
Chirstopher Kelly appeared via zoom for Joanne Karetzky (petitioner).
Jeffrey Neustadt appeared in person for Paul Bellastrasse (trustee).

Discussion was held regarding the suspension of the trustee, that an accounting filing dating back to 2017 Court order has not been complied with, and that electrical work on a property has not been performed to get the home insured. The Court advises counsel to amend the petition to only address the survivor's trust and file a separate petition for the bypass trust. The Court also orders the suspension of Paul Bellastrasse as trustee and appoints Joanne Karetzky as temporary successor trustee. Status on trust and Status on accounting preparation are set for March 04, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The matter is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross Moody. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Singh. Angel Pourtabib (Court Reporter) csr#13714, 415-601-7054, apourtabib@gmail.com.

Petition Of Beneficiary To Compel Trustee To Account
Off Calendar Jul-10-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian McDonald for and with petitioner Joanne Karetzky; Jon Vaught for Respondent Paul Ballestrasse.

Mr. Vaught reported that his client is working on the accounting right now, but since the accounting covers a period of ten years there has been a delay in getting all the necessary bank records. He stated that he is expecting receipt of records from the Bank of America soon. He requested the hearing be continued to September to give time to complete the accounting. Mr. McDonald replied that he would like the accounting to be complete, but that he wanted a shorter period of continuance to be given. In response to the Court's inquiry, Mr. McDonald stated that the relief sought by the petition was the giving of an accounting, not the filing of a petition for approval of the accounting. The Court granted the petition and ordered the accounting to be provided to the petitioner by August 22, 2017. Mr. McDonald agreed to prepare and circulate for approval as to form an order in conformity with the Court's ruling granting the petition subject to receipt and review of the order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)
